Title: IRBuilder Class Reference
URL: https://llvm-swift.github.io/LLVMSwift/Classes/IRBuilder.html
Content: A prominent example of this is the PHI node. Building a PHI node produces an empty PHI node. A PHI node must have its incoming basic blocks attached. let

Title: Building and using llvmlite - a basic example - Eli Bendersky's website
URL: https://eli.thegreenplace.net/2015/building-and-using-llvmlite-a-basic-example
Content: llvmlite was created last year by the developers of Numba (a JIT compiler for scientific Python), and just recently replaced llvmpy as their bridge to LLVM. One of the reasons the Numba devs decided to ditch llvmpy in favor of a new approach is the biggest issue heavy users of LLVM as a library have - its incredible rate of API-breaking change. The LLVM C++ API is notoriously unstable and will remain this way for the foreseeable future. Since building the IR using repeated FFI calls to LLVM proved to be slow and error-prone in llvmpy, llvmlite re-implemented the IR builder in pure Python. This also reduces the "API surface" llvmlite uses, since the textual representation of LLVM IR is one of the more stable things in LLVM. LLVM has a great tool named llvm-config in its binary image, and the Makefile in llvmlite uses it, which means building llvmlite with any version of LLVM I want is just a simple matter of running:.

Title: IR builders — llvmlite 0.48.0dev0+54.ge8d38ef.dirty documentation
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user-guide/ir/ir-builder.html
Content: It allows you to fill the basic blocks of your functions with LLVM instructions. An `IRBuilder` internally maintains a current basic block and a pointer inside the block’s list of instructions. When a new instruction is added, it is inserted at that point, and then the pointer is advanced after the new instruction. A `IRBuilder` also maintains a reference to metadata describing the current source location, which is attached to all inserted instructions. If *block*—a `Block`—is given, the builder starts at the end of this basic block. :   The basic block that the builder is operating on. :   The function that the builder is operating on. :   Append a basic block, with the given optional *name*, to the current function. The current block is also changed to the instruction’s basic block. :   Position at the end of the basic *block*. :   Position the builder either at the end of the basic *block*, if it is not terminated, or just before the *block*’s terminator:.
